meta.proposal_source: idea-engine control/outbox.md @ main 99ce912768eea541aef7a8c863b91147cfd7d10c (landed via idea-engine PR #402)
meta.hermetic: the runner reads ONLY this fixtures.json; zero repo/network reads at verdict time; every constant below copied verbatim from the PROPOSAL 059 block / idea doc

milestones.semantics: earned once, kept forever (survive resets); ms_pct = 100 + 5 * earned; owned rungs structurally unreachable at count 1 (asserted in-sim)

policies.name: fixed-1
policies.note: m=1 IS the committed greedy policy (reset at lifetime >= 100000)

policy_semantics.fixed: reset at the first boundary with run lifetime >= m*100000
policy_semantics.hybrid: greedy (fixed-1), except ONE patient run to 10000000 triggered at the first reset boundary (run lifetime >= 100000) with P >= k and the detour unused; the patient run resets at the first boundary with lifetime >= 10000000 (award isqrt(lifetime//100000) at the actual boundary lifetime); greedy thereafter
policy_semantics.cooldown: greedy, but a reset is legal only once the run is >= tau seconds old (reset at the first boundary with lifetime >= 100000 AND age >= tau)

decision.metric: total(pi) = cumulative primary PRODUCED over [0,H], exact integer (sum of all crediting across all runs; spending never subtracts)
decision.reject: exists pi in Pi \ {fixed-1}: 100*total(pi) >= 101*total(fixed-1), exact big-int, Arm A reproduced exactly by Arm B
decision.invalid: any F1-F6 gate fails — report, no ruling
decision.approve: forall pi in Pi \ {fixed-1}: 100*total(pi) <= 99*total(fixed-1) AND total(fixed-1) >= 2*total(never)
null_axes: mechanism-miss (REJECT fires but the beater also beats with milestones OFF)
horizon-conditional (H/2 leg lands the promoted clause on the other side — named, never ruling)
twin-arm disagreement surviving the INVALID diagnosis

arm_r.order: all hybrid triggers first, then all cooldowns, one RNG stream per seed
arm_r.role: REPORTING-ONLY — no statistical gate rides Arm R; a probe beating the Pi-best is a named finding, never a ruling; every probe total must re-evaluate EXACTLY through Arm B

F2.note: 80795 is HARD under THIS spec's pinned boundary order; the vendored harness's convention counts 80796 (the disclosed +-1 AMB-convention difference, see choice C1)

F3.rule: total_OFF(pi) <= total_ON(pi) for every pi (exact monotonicity), and fixed-1 OFF reproduces F2's anchors

F4.rule: balance + cumulative upgrade spend == run lifetime at every boundary; sum of closed run lifetimes + open run lifetime == total at every boundary; sum(owned) == 1 always (owned rungs never fire)

F6.rule: twin evaluators exact-equal on every total (ON@H, OFF@H, ON@H/2, Arm-R main, Arm-R stability); randrange sentinels 12+6 on 20261373 and 4+2 on 20261374 counted and asserted; aux 20261376 never read; stdout + results.json byte-identical across two process runs (external sha256); CPython minor pinned 3.11

quoted_sentences_pinned_at_5ddd5a2.text: doubling a run does not double the award, so the optimal loop is reset-and-grow rather than one endless grind
the optimal loop is reset-and-regrow, not one endless grind — the core prestige feel
S3 — optimal-play speedrun … Policy per second matches S2's visit order: credit, greedy-buy, prestige iff eligible
optimal play reaches ~80,796 resets in 14 days (late resets ~13 s each) — uncovered by any criterion, likely wants a pre-registered cap/cooldown criterion in v2 of the doc

fixture_level_choices_disclosed_before_r.C1: Horizon-boundary convention: the boundary sequence (award/buy/award/reset/award) runs only when the post-credit time t < H; an event landing exactly at t == H credits but takes no boundary actions. This is the convention under which the registered F2 hard anchor 80,795 holds; the vendored harness runs actions at an event landing exactly at H and counts 80,796 — a reset lands exactly at t = 1,209,600 in the milestones-OFF greedy world (the PROPOSAL's disclosed +-1 AMB-convention difference). Policy TOTALS are convention-independent (no crediting occurs after H).
fixture_level_choices_disclosed_before_r.C2: Arm A event set: next-purchase affordability crossing, next unearned lifetime-milestone crossing (milestones-ON worlds only), and the policy reset event (fixed/hybrid: lifetime-target crossing; cooldown: max(lifetime-crossing, run_start + tau)). Prestige and owned milestone progress change only AT boundaries, so they need no events. The full pinned boundary sequence runs at every event boundary; between boundaries the rate is a constant integer, so jumping ceil(need/rate) seconds equals 1-second ticking (the harness's documented equivalence, here proven by the per-second twin).
fixture_level_choices_disclosed_before_r.C3: hybrid-k trigger: at the first boundary where run lifetime >= 100,000, if the detour is unused and P >= k, the run continues (no reset, detour armed) to the 10,000,000 target and resets at the first boundary with lifetime >= 10,000,000, award computed on the actual boundary lifetime; greedy thereafter. k = 0 arms the detour at the very first reset boundary.
fixture_level_choices_disclosed_before_r.C4: Mechanism-miss (NULL axis ii) is evaluated on the ARGMAX beater pi* (the best-total policy among those firing the REJECT inequality): the class demotes to NULL(mechanism-miss) iff the REJECT inequality fires AND 100*total_OFF(pi*) >= 101*total_OFF(fixed-1); attribution to the milestone fold holds iff the best beater's beat disappears with milestones OFF.
fixture_level_choices_disclosed_before_r.C5: Sentinels count randrange() invocations via a counting Random subclass: exactly 12 then 6 on seed 20261373, exactly 4 then 2 on seed 20261374 (per-phase counts asserted). Seed 20261375 performs exactly ONE shuffle of the presentation row order of the 15-policy table (reporting only; results.json carries canonical order plus the shuffled name list). Aux seed 20261376: asserted never constructed (the constructed-RNG set is exactly {20261373, 20261374, 20261375}).
fixture_level_choices_disclosed_before_r.C6: Reset-time series serialization: per-day reset counts (14 bins, day = t // 86400 of the reset boundary), first 3 and last 3 reset records (t, duration, award, lifetime, level), min/max/final run durations, and sha256 of the full comma-joined reset-time series (full series ~175k entries for greedy-class policies — the hash pins it; committing it verbatim would bloat the ledger).
fixture_level_choices_disclosed_before_r.C7: Serialization: every decision number is an exact integer; ratios are reported as exact 'num/den' strings plus repr-float conveniences; results.json written with sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=True, single trailing newline.
fixture_level_choices_disclosed_before_r.C8: F4 conservation is checked at EVERY boundary as integer equalities (balance + run-spend == run-lifetime; closed-run lifetime sum + open-run lifetime == running total; owned is the constant 1 by construction with the owned-rung unreachability asserted), aggregated into a per-policy-run boolean.
fixture_level_choices_disclosed_before_r.C9: REJECT's 'both arms' clause is implemented as: the inequality holds on Arm A totals AND Arm B reproduces every ON-grid@H total exactly (twin equality on the decision leg; also required by F6 across all legs).
